name: I got sick from breathing chemicals at work in Murray Utah, what do I do first?
description: File a workers' comp claim right away, document exposure details, see a doctor for a work-related diagnosis, and report to your employer in writing.

mainEntity.name: I got sick from breathing chemicals at work in Murray Utah, what do I do first?

acceptedAnswer.text: File a workers' comp claim right away, document exposure details, see a doctor for a work-related diagnosis, and report to your employer in writing.
acceptedAnswer.description:
**File a workers' compensation claim immediately with your employer.**

Workplace respiratory exposure often qualifies as a work-related injury covered by workers' comp in Utah, providing medical benefits and wage replacement without proving fault[1][2]. Employers must follow OSHA rules for safe conditions, including PPE and hazard warnings; failure can strengthen your claim[1].

- **Document everything**: Note exposure date, location, chemicals involved, symptoms, and witnesses.
- **Seek medical care**: Get a doctor's diagnosis linking symptoms to work exposure.
- **Report to employer in writing** and notify OSHA if hazards persist.
- **Avoid signing anything** without understanding.

Notify your employer within Utah's 180-day limit and consult a workers' comp attorney to file properly. Act quickly to preserve benefits[1][2].
